name: Ithink Financial
description_short: iTHINK Financial is a Florida-based credit union with $1.5B in assets, 95,000+ members, and 22 branches across Florida and Georgia offering full-service banking.
description_long:
iTHINK Financial was founded in 1969 as IBM Boca Raton Employees' Federal Credit Union when IBM employees each deposited 25 cents to create a member-owned financial institution. The credit union expanded its membership eligibility over decades—from IBM employees only, to all IBM employees nationally, to employees of multiple companies, and eventually to anyone living in approved counties or with family connections to existing members. In 2017 and 2019, iTHINK Financial acquired Mackinac Savings Bank and Oculina Bank respectively, expanding its branch footprint significantly. The organization rebranded to iTHINK Financial in March 2020 and currently operates as a Florida state-chartered, federally insured credit union with headquarters in Delray Beach, Florida.

As a not-for-profit credit union, iTHINK Financial offers comprehensive banking services including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit, IRAs, health savings accounts, credit cards, vehicle loans, mortgages, business loans, and online/mobile banking. Members also have access to investment services and insurance products. The credit union employs approximately 380 staff members across its 22-branch network and serves over 95,000 members.

What distinguishes iTHINK Financial is its credit union structure, which means profits are returned to members through lower fees, better loan rates, and higher dividends compared to traditional banks. The organization maintains NCUA federal insurance protection covering savings up to at least $250,000 per member. The dual acquisition strategy in 2017-2019 demonstrates growth-focused management and expanded service capabilities beyond typical credit union offerings. As an equal housing lender, iTHINK Financial explicitly commits to fair lending practices.

Potential members should note that membership eligibility remains restricted—you must work for or be retired from a member company, live in an approved county, worship/attend school in an approved county, or be related to an existing member. While the Wikipedia article indicates current asset levels and branch counts, some details lack recent citations (as flagged by Wikipedia editors in November 2019), so prospective members should verify current information directly with the credit union. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Savings accounts and money market accounts
pros: Not-for-profit structure returns earnings to members as lower fees and better rates than traditional banks
NCUA federal insurance covers deposits up to at least $250,000 per member
Comprehensive product suite includes checking, savings, mortgages, business loans, credit cards, and investment services
Significant asset base of $1.5 billion provides stability and resources
22 branches across Florida and Georgia plus online and mobile banking access
Equal housing lender committed to fair lending practices
Expanded service capabilities through strategic acquisitions of Mackinac Savings Bank and Oculina Bank
cons: Membership eligibility is restricted—you cannot join unless you work for a member company, live in an approved county, have family ties to a member, or attend school/worship in an approved county
Wikipedia article lacks recent citations (flagged November 2019), suggesting some published information may be outdated
With 22 branches, geographic reach is limited primarily to Florida and Georgia versus nationwide banking options
As a credit union, product offerings and terms may vary compared to larger national banks with more standardized rates
Limited online presence and marketing compared to major national financial institutions
best_for: IBM employees and retirees seeking member-owned banking with below-market rates
Employees of other member companies in Florida and Georgia wanting not-for-profit banking benefits
Individuals living in approved Florida or Georgia counties looking for lower-fee checking and savings accounts
Members seeking mortgage and auto loan products with credit union-level rates and terms
diagnosis: iTHINK Financial is best for employees of member companies or residents of approved Florida/Georgia counties who qualify for membership and want not-for-profit banking with lower fees and competitive loan rates. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted by eligibility criteria—you cannot open an account simply by choosing the credit union; you must meet specific employment, residency, or family relationship requirements.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
